subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: An auto insurance company insures an automobile worth 15,000 for one year under a policy with a 1,000 deductible. During the policy year there is a 0.04 chance of partial damage to the car and a 0.02 chance of a total loss of the car. If there is partial damage to the car, the amount $X$ of damage (in thousands) follows a distribution with density function $f(x) = \begin{cases} 0.5003 e^{-0.5x} & 0 < x < 15 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise.} \end{cases}$ What is the expected claim payment?
